info.name: HGN IAM — Platform Full Testing
info.description:
Full AuthZ testing collection aligned with V2/V3/V4 seeds.

Run order:
1) 00 - Logins (all users)
2) 01-05 - SuperAdmin-only resources (Permissions, Scopes, Role Hierarchy, Permission Groups, Policies)
3) 06 - Roles (Delegated creation)
4) 07 - Org Admin User Lifecycle (Create User → Assign Role → Verify)
5) 08 - Assignments (Delegated, with cross-scope 403 tests)
6) 09 - Deny Rules (Delegated, with wildcard and cross-scope 403 tests)
7) 10 - Audit (Senior admins only)
8) 11 - AuthZ Runtime (Service-to-Service + User-Facing)
9) 12 - Access Denied Scenarios

Org Scope Hierarchy:
  GLOBAL -> ASIA_PACIFIC -> NEPAL -+-> Nepal Operations
                                   +-> Nepal Travel Agency
                                   +-> Nepal Sales Agency
                                   +-> Nepal Rescue Centre
                                   +-> Nepal Rescue Company
                                   +-> Nepal Insurance Company
                                   +-> Nepal Hospital
                                   +-> Nepal Service Provider
                                   +-> Nepal Government Bodies

Key Rule:
- SuperAdmin: can do everything (bypasses all delegation checks)
- CountryAdmin: can manage all Nepal sub-scopes + has broad permissions
- OrgAdmins: can manage within their own scope only, within their permission ceiling
- Wildcard deny rules: SuperAdmin only
- Permissions/Scopes/Policies/RoleHierarchy/PermissionGroups: SuperAdmin only

item.name: 01 - Permissions (SuperAdmin Only)
item.description: Permissions are production-hardcoded. Only SuperAdmin can create/view them. All other roles get 403.

item.name: [SuperAdmin CAN] List all permissions (capture first ID)
02 - Scopes (SuperAdmin Only)
item.description: Scope hierarchy is production-hardcoded. Only SuperAdmin can create/manage scopes.

item.name: [SuperAdmin CAN] Get descendants of NEPAL (all 9 orgs)
[SuperAdmin CAN] Get descendants of GLOBAL (all scopes under global)
[SuperAdmin CAN] Create new scope (INDIA country under ASIA_PACIFIC)
[SuperAdmin CAN] Add hierarchy (TravelAgencyManager inherits from TravelAgencyStaff)
[SuperAdmin CAN] Get permission group by ID

body.raw: {"name": "TA Order Approval Policy", "description": "Allow order approval by Travel Agency managers", "permissionKey": "order.order.approve", "resourceType": "order", "scopeId": "00000000-0000-0000-0001-000000000002", "effect": "ALLOW", "priority": 10, "conditions": {"org_type": "TRAVEL_AGENCY"}, "active": true, "createdBy": "superadmin@hgn.com"}

item.name: 06 - Roles (Delegated)
item.description: Role creation is delegated: org admins can create roles WITHIN their permission ceiling. SuperAdmin can create any role.

item.name: [Any Auth] List roles by org type (TRAVEL_AGENCY)
[SuperAdmin CAN] Create role with any permissions
[TravelAdmin CAN] Create role within permission ceiling (order.order.* permissions only)

body.raw: {"name": "TravelBookingAgent", "displayName": "Travel Booking Agent", "description": "Agent who can create and read travel orders", "orgType": "TRAVEL_AGENCY", "permissionIds": []}

request.description: TravelAgencyAdmin can create roles with permissions they hold. Passing empty permissionIds to avoid needing to look up actual IDs.

item.name: [OperationsAdmin CAN] Create role within ceiling (operations domain)
07 — Org Admin: Create User → Assign Role → Verify Access
item.description:
TravelAgencyAdmin creates a new staff member account, assigns TravelAgencyStaff role within their org scope, and verifies the user can see their scope and permissions.

The test@hgn.com user (pre-seeded, no role) is also used to demonstrate assignment by TravelAdmin.

request.description: Self-registration creates an unverified account. In dev, check server logs for the email verification token or use /api/auth/resend-verification. After verification, the user can log in.

item.name: [Step 2] Resend verification to new staff (if needed)

request.description: Triggers re-send of verification email. Check dev console/logs for the verification token.

item.name: [Step 3 - TravelAdmin CAN] Assign TravelAgencyStaff to test@hgn.com at TravelAgency scope

request.description: TravelAgencyAdmin assigns TravelAgencyStaff role to the test user within their own org scope. The scope containment check passes because travelAgencyScopeId is within TravelAdmin's authority.

item.name: [Step 4 - TravelAdmin CAN] View assignments for test user
[Step 5 - TestUser CAN] Check my scopes (should see TravelAgency)

request.description: After TravelAdmin assigned a role, test user should see TravelAgency scope in their scope list.

exec: pm.test('Has at least one scope', () => pm.response.json().data.length >= 1);

item.name: [Step 6 - TestUser CAN] Check my permissions at TravelAgency scope

request.description: TravelAgencyStaff has: order.order.create/read, order.item.create/read, document.file.upload/read, notification.message.send/read, user.profile.read

item.name: [Step 7 - TravelAdmin CAN] Assign TravelAgencyManager to OperationsOfficer at TravelAgency scope

request.description: Cross-org assignment: TravelAdmin assigns a secondary role to OperationsOfficer within TravelAgency scope. This is allowed since TravelAdmin has authority over travelAgencyScope.

item.name: [Step 8 - TravelAdmin CAN] Revoke assignment from test user
[Step 9 - TestUser CAN] Check my scopes again (should be empty now)
[TravelAdmin CANNOT] Assign role at NEPAL scope (cross-scope) → 403

request.description: TravelAdmin does NOT have authority over NEPAL scope. Scope containment check fails → 403 Forbidden.
TravelAgencyAdmin does not hold CountryAdmin permissions, so cannot grant that role. Permission ceiling check fails → 403 Forbidden.

item.name: [CountryAdmin CAN] Assign TravelAgencyAdmin to test user at Nepal scope

request.description: CountryAdmin has authority over all Nepal child scopes (scope containment) and holds all permissions TravelAgencyAdmin has (permission ceiling). This should succeed.

exec: pm.test('Status 201 - CountryAdmin can delegate to org scope', () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));

item.name: [CountryAdmin CAN] List assignments for Nepal country admin
[SuperAdmin CAN] Assign SuperAdmin role to test user at Global scope
[RescueCentreAdmin CAN] Assign RescueDispatcher to rescue operator at RescueCentre scope

request.description: RescueCentreAdmin assigns RescueCentreAdmin role to test user within their org scope.

item.name: [InsuranceAdmin CAN] Assign InsuranceReviewer to test user at Insurance scope
[OperationsAdmin CAN] Assign OperationsOfficer at Operations scope
[SuperAdmin CAN] List deny rules for test user
[SuperAdmin CAN] Create deny rule (order.order.delete) for test user at TravelAgency
[SuperAdmin CAN] Create wildcard deny rule (order.*.delete) for test user

request.description: Wildcard deny rules (* patterns) can only be created by SuperAdmin.

item.name: [TravelAdmin CAN] Create deny rule for test user at TravelAgency scope

request.description: TravelAgencyAdmin has authority over travelAgencyScope, so can create deny rules within it.
Wildcard deny rules require SuperAdmin. TravelAdmin gets 403.

item.name: [TravelAdmin CANNOT] Create deny rule at NEPAL scope → 403

request.description: TravelAdmin does not have authority over NEPAL scope. Scope containment check fails → 403.

item.name: [SuperAdmin CAN] Get audit by subject (superAdmin himself)
[SuperAdmin CAN] Get audit statistics for test user (7 days)
[Service CAN] Authorize - SuperAdmin order.order.delete at TravelAgency
[Service CAN] Authorize - TravelAdmin order.order.approve at TravelAgency

exec: pm.test('TravelAdmin can approve orders', () => pm.response.json().authorized === true);
pm.test('RescueCentreAdmin can respond to SOS', () => pm.response.json().authorized === true);
pm.test('InsuranceAdmin can approve claims', () => pm.response.json().authorized === true);
pm.test('OperationsOfficer can read SOS', () => pm.response.json().authorized === true);

item.name: [Service] Effective permissions - TravelAdmin at TravelAgency scope
[Service] Effective permissions - RescueCompanyAdmin at RescueCompany scope
[User] Get my permissions at TravelAgency - TravelAdmin
[User] Get my permissions at RescueCentre - RescueCentreAdmin
[User] Get my permissions at Operations - OperationsOfficer
[User] Get my permissions at Insurance - InsuranceReviewer
12 - Access Denied Scenarios
item.description: Explicit tests that verify unauthorized operations return 403. Covers cross-scope and super-admin-only operations.

exec: pm.test('Status 403 - org admin cannot create permissions', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
pm.test('Status 403 - org admin cannot create scopes', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
pm.test('Status 403 - org admin cannot create policies', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

item.name: [DENY] SalesAdmin → assign role at Operations scope (cross-org)

exec: pm.test('Status 403 - SalesAdmin cannot assign at Operations scope', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

item.name: [DENY] HospitalAdmin → assign role at RescueCentre scope (cross-org)

exec: pm.test('Status 403 - HospitalAdmin cannot assign at RescueCentre scope', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

item.name: [DENY] OperationsAdmin → create deny rule at TravelAgency scope

exec: pm.test('Status 403 - OperationsAdmin cannot create deny rule at TravelAgency', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
